skill.name: skill-seekers
skill.description: Guidance for turning documentation websites, GitHub repositories, and PDFs into Claude AI skill plans.
skill.summary: Plan Claude skills from documentation, repositories, and PDF source material.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two hardcoded URLs and two weak cryptography patterns. The URLs are source repository references, and the weak cryptography matches appear to be false positives in descriptive text rather than crypto usage. No prompt injection, executable code, or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed SKILL.md file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography indicators at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20. These lines contain descriptive skill text and no cryptographic algorithm, key handling, hashing, encryption, or code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain markdown and YAML description text. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic operation in those locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Repository URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes hardcoded GitHub URLs in metadata and documentation. These links do not initiate network requests, but they point users to an external repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible in metadata and markdown. They are documentation links, so the security impact is low.

content.value_statement: Teams often have useful documentation spread across sites, repositories, and PDFs. This skill helps frame those sources as Claude skill material with clear scope and usage guidance.
actual_capabilities: Identifies documentation websites, GitHub repositories, and PDFs as supported source types.
Explains when to use the skill for Claude AI skill creation work.
Provides high-level guidance and patterns for planning a skill from source material.
Links to the upstream source repository for additional context.
Works as a lightweight prompt guide for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users.
limitations: Does not include executable conversion scripts or parsers.
Does not define a full file generation workflow inside SKILL.md.
Requires users to inspect source material and validate generated skill content.
External repository links may change outside this skill package.

use_cases.title: Plan a Skill From Product Docs
use_cases.description: Turn a documentation site into a focused Claude skill outline with scope, inputs, and expected responses.

use_cases.title: Prepare Repository Knowledge
use_cases.description: Use repository documentation as source material for a reusable AI assistant workflow.

use_cases.title: Review Source Fit Before Publishing
use_cases.description: Check whether PDFs or web documents contain enough stable guidance to support a marketplace skill.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Skill Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this documentation source and propose a Claude skill outline. Include the skill purpose, target users, supported inputs, and clear limitations.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Reusable Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the key procedures from this repository or PDF into concise skill instructions. Keep only stable guidance that an AI assistant can follow.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Source Materials
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these documentation sources for skill readiness. Rank them by completeness, stability, security risk, and usefulness for Claude skill creation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Draft Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft Claude skill against its source material. Identify missing instructions, unsupported claims, security concerns, and marketplace content gaps.

output_examples.input: A documentation site for an internal deployment process.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with audience, workflow steps, required context, expected outputs, and limitations.

output_examples.input: A GitHub repository with README files and usage guides.
output: A list of instructions that need human validation before publishing.

output_examples.input: A PDF manual for a product support workflow.
output_examples.output: A structured conversion brief that separates stable support rules from content that needs current verification.

best_practices: Use source material that is stable, well organized, and clearly licensed.
Keep generated skill instructions narrow enough for reliable assistant behavior.
Review every external link and claim before publishing a marketplace listing.
anti_patterns: Do not claim the skill performs automated conversion when no converter is included.
Do not include secrets, private repository data, or restricted PDFs in public skills.
Do not publish generated instructions without checking them against the original source.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically convert files?
faq.answer: No. The included SKILL.md provides guidance, not an executable converter.

faq.question: Can it work with GitHub repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill description names GitHub repositories as a supported source type for planning skill content.

faq.question: Can it process PDFs directly?
faq.answer: The skill can guide planning from PDF content, but it does not include a PDF parser.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with low risk. The reviewed file contains only descriptive guidance and external repository links.

faq.question: What should users verify before use?
faq.answer: Users should verify source accuracy, licensing, external links, and any generated marketplace claims.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The package is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatibility.
